critical_failures: Form inputs without labels or ARIA

Missing nonce verification / permission check: This is a REST API method that restores plugin defaults (a destructive state-changing operation) but contains no capability check (e.g., current_user_can()) or nonce/REST permission enforcement visible within the function itself. The function signature accepts $parameters but never validates authorization.
Suppressed error with @ operator on opcache_invalidate() — silencing errors is an anti-pattern and hides file-system failures.
Uses direct PHP file functions (copy()) instead of WP_Filesystem API for file operations.
md5(gmdate('H:i:s') . wp_rand()) is a weak secret generation pattern — gmdate is low-entropy; should use wp_generate_password() or random_bytes() for security tokens.
The $parameters argument is accepted but never used or validated, suggesting incomplete implementation.
